title: Betrayal at House on the Hill

component_identification.content: Modular room tiles (ground, upper, basement), character cards and sliders for four traits (Might, Speed, Knowledge, Sanity), omen/item/event cards, dice, tokens, scenario books (Traitor Tome and Secrets of Survival), and assorted monster/traitor markers. Players each use one explorer with trait sliders that move up and down as they take effects.

core_game_loop.content: The game has two phases. In the Explore phase, players move through the mansion revealing new room tiles and resolving card draws from symbols on those rooms. When enough omens have appeared, a haunt roll may fail, triggering the Haunt phase. One player (or occasionally all) becomes the traitor based on the discovered omen and room, and the game shifts to asymmetric objectives until one side wins.

detailed_rules.content: On your turn you may move up to your current Speed. Entering an unexplored doorway forces you to place a legal room tile of that floor and continue movement if able. If the revealed room has an Event, Item, or Omen symbol, draw that card type and resolve immediately. Most checks roll a number of custom dice equal to a trait value and compare to a target number. Attacks are usually Might vs Might; damage lowers traits by moving sliders down. After each omen draw, make a haunt roll by rolling six dice and trying to roll higher than the number of omen cards revealed. If you fail, consult the haunt chart using the omen and room combination to determine scenario number. The traitor reads the matching entry in Traitor Tome; heroes read Secrets of Survival. Hidden information and different win conditions begin. During haunt play, follow the scenario-specific setup and special rules exactly, including monster behavior, timers, and banes.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Betrayal does not use points. The game ends when either the hero team or traitor side fulfills the scenario win condition. Common objectives include exorcisms, escapes, collecting items, surviving rounds, or defeating a key character/monster. If your relevant trait reaches skull on its track, your explorer dies unless scenario text overrides it.

beginner_strategy.content: Explore quickly before the haunt so your team finds useful rooms and items. Keep explorers somewhat grouped so low-stat characters are not isolated when the traitor reveal happens. Preserve weak traits, especially Sanity and Might, by avoiding optional risky effects when behind. Read scenario text carefully after the haunt; many losses happen from missing a specific timing or setup clause. As heroes, share information openly and focus on objective progress instead of chasing the traitor unless required.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 527; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
